subject: Sociology
title: Durkheim on Totemism
description: Learn Durkheim on Totemism in one minute.

hook.text: Aboriginal clans worshipped animals as sacred. Durkheim saw something bigger hiding beneath the rituals.

buildup.text: He studied totemism — the simplest known religion — to uncover what all religions share at their core.

discovery.text: The totem represents the clan itself. When people worship it, they're really worshipping their own society.

twist.text: This means God, for Durkheim, is society in disguise. The divine is the collective felt as greater.

climax.text: Religion is society worshipping itself. The sacred power people feel is the force of the group.

punchline.text: We worship the group without knowing.

quiz.question: What did Durkheim conclude about totemism?
options: People were worshipping their own society
